comment: "As I'm a final year medical student, who is currently on internship rotations (exposed to hand surgery rotation with a massive amount of surgical procedures and cases), the most interesting part for me is the flap reconstruction and thumb replantation with microsurgery (I have assisted in a thumb replantation and took 5 hours to complete, but i didn't have much of knowledge as it was an emergency case). After attending this course I'm confident with my knowledge."
"Very good course in general. I knew most of the subjects but some tips were new to me in the management on tendon, nerve repair and flaps."
"The course instructor was very knowledgeable. The information was presented in a very concise yet very informative way. I am very grateful for the learning opportunity."
"The most interesting aspects of the Hand Trauma Course were the detailed tendon repair techniques, flap reconstruction options, and clinical decision-making based on injury zones."
"Everthing about the hand reconstruction is explained in an organized way. Supporting the topic with cases is helpful to figure out the procedures and the techniques."

"I love the videos and the slow presentation of the modules. What I found most interesting was the applications of tissue flap reconstructive process and the application of nerve and vascular repair. Another useful point was the presenters experience of dealing with difficult traumatic injuries that are quite common in practice, the operative order of tackling these first and second level reconstructions to simplify reconstruction for the operative practitioner is valuable."
"I think this course is very informative and included very interesting cases. Also good speaker. I really appreciate your efforts."
